name: Building Edge
paraphrase: Treat the edge of the building as a 'thing', a 'place', a zone with volume to it, not a line or interface that has no thickness. Alexander argues that buildings with sharp, sheer edges sterilise the ground around them, while a well-developed edge - made of arcades, terraces, galleries, balconies, alcoves and benches where indoor and outdoor life overlap - invites passers-by to linger and the occupants to spill outward.
